senses.definition: (of a person's facial expression or mood) to suddenly become unhappy, worried, or troubled

examples.x: Her face clouded over when she received the unexpected news of her friend's accident.
The joyous atmosphere at the party quickly changed as his expression clouded over with concern.
He was laughing and joking, but his mood suddenly clouded over when he received a disappointing phone call.
The teacher's face clouded over when she noticed that her students hadn't completed the assigned homework.
As he recounted the traumatic experience, his expression clouded over with sadness and fear.

senses.definition: (of bodies of water or other surfaces) to become completely covered or blocked with ice due to extremely cold temperatures

examples.x: The lake froze over during the harsh winter, allowing people to ice skate on its surface.
The river froze over, creating a thick layer of ice that was dangerous to cross.
The small pond in the park usually freezes over by December.
After a night of sub-zero temperatures, the car's windshield had frozen over.
The sidewalk froze over, making it treacherous for pedestrians.

senses.definition: to show excessive or unnecessary concern, care, or attention to someone or something

examples.x: Sarah tends to fuss over her children, making sure they have everything they need and more.
The chef fussed over every detail of the meal to ensure it was perfect.
Don't fuss over me; I can take care of myself.
He fussed over his appearance for hours before the important job interview.
My grandmother always fussed over her garden, making sure every flower was just right.

senses.definition: to recover from an unpleasant or unhappy experience, particularly an illness

examples.x: It took her several weeks to get over the flu completely.
With proper treatment, most people can get over a common cold within a week.
The therapy sessions helped her get over the trauma from the accident.
After the breakup, it took him a long time to get over the pain.
She finally got over her fear of public speaking.

senses.definition: (of a threat, problem, concern, etc.) to exist and create a sense of worry or uncertainty about what will happen

examples.x: The threat of a looming deadline hung over the project, adding pressure to complete it on time.
The financial crisis continued to hang over the economy, affecting investment and spending.
The pending exam results hung over the students, making it difficult for them to focus on other tasks.
The unresolved conflict between the two countries hung over diplomatic discussions, making negotiations challenging.
The possibility of layoffs was hanging over the employees, causing anxiety throughout the company.

senses.definition: (of wounds) to slowly grow new skin over the injured area as part of the healing process

examples.x: The cut on his knee took a few weeks to heal over completely.
The doctor assured her that the surgical incision would heal over in time.
To prevent infection, make sure the wound is clean and dry as it heals over.
The scab will gradually fall off as the skin underneath continues to heal over.
It's important not to pick at the scab to allow the wound to heal over properly.

senses.definition: to significantly transform the appearance of something to update, enhance, or modernize its look

examples.x: She made over her bedroom by painting the walls a new color and adding new furniture.
The restaurant made over its menu to include more vegetarian and vegan options.
The company made over their website to make it more user-friendly.
The team made over their old office space into a modern workspace.

senses.definition: to have a thin layer of mist or water droplets cover a surface, creating a hazy or obscured appearance

examples.x: As the temperature dropped, the window began to mist over, creating a hazy view of the landscape.
As the hot coffee met the cold glass, the surface began to mist over.
The bathroom mirror misted over after the warm shower, making it necessary to wipe away the condensation.
In the early hours of dawn, the valley misted over, giving it an ethereal and enchanting appearance.
During the cool morning, the lake misted over, creating a serene and mysterious atmosphere.

senses.definition: to happen without really affecting or bothering someone

examples.x: The criticism seemed to wash over them, leaving them focused on their objectives.
The challenges in the project appeared to wash over the experienced team.
The news of the setback seemed to wash over him, leaving him unfazed.

senses.definition: to get covered with a layer of ice, due to freezing conditions

examples.x: The roads tend to ice over quickly during winter storms, making driving dangerous.
Be careful walking on the sidewalks; they've iced over after the rain froze overnight.
We couldn't fish on the river today because it had iced over completely.
